reviews.review_text: EV chargers if you have an electric vehicle to charge overnight. I wouldn't particularly recommend tent sleeping due to the highway noise.
Lovely by the Brunswick River & close enough to walk into town for local  cuisine & atmosphere
Can’t beat this place
Beautiful location and sunset! Clean facilities.

Well what a great park, clean and ample toilets, showers and Laundry. Very friendly staff. Well positioned and easy walk or bike ride into Brunswick Heads. Double slab site made it very clean especially as it rained for the bulk of our stay. We'll be back for sure.

Great clean and spacious park. Lot's of room between vans. The staff are very friendly.  The park is a little bit away from town, but that's the joy's of travelling with your fur baby.  Its a easy flat walk into town, about 20 minutes along the river. Yes, you do hear some highway traffic but nothing that would stop me from booking again. You can swim in the river, there's some good sandy spots to get in. I'll definitely be rebooking when the weather gets warmer.

We stayed here for 2 nights in our van with friends in a caravan. Will be marking as a favourite. Yes there is highway noise, but I knew this when I booked - looked on Google Maps.  Lovely easy walk into Brunswick Heads for coffee and the hot fresh doughnut stall. Super dog friendly and my old girl loved it.  The amenities was one of the cleanest I have seen.  So we will definitely be back to stay again.
Had a lovely stay. Sitting on the deck with views to the river were very relaxing. We are planning our next stay as we enjoyed it so much
Excellent a short walk from our cabin to go fishing in the Brunswick heads river

Great location and site.
Amenities perfect
Staff so helpful, location so beautiful. Best park in the Northern Rivers.

Lovely tiny home to stay in right on Brunswick river. The home is pet friendly and was a good size outdoor fenced area secure with roof covering for you and your pets. The bed is only a double so if you are used to a bigger bed get ready to be squishy. Lots of bins around the park and doggie bag dispensers. There’s a nice walk close to the park as well. Heaps of people fishing along the river. And people paddling down the Brunswick river.
Always a fantastic stay. Thank you Carly and her team.

We have stayed at this park before and were expecting a little more from the Tiny Home. Value for money at peak holiday rates was not there. Overgrown shrubs in the parking space, made it very difficult to get in and out of our vehicle each day. The shower head fell off constantly and shower door needed adjusting also, as you had to slam it shut. 2 towels for a 4 night stay was not enough. Windows and floors needed a good clean, as well as the outdoor area. We needed to squish a few cockroaches too.
Great place, clean facilities, an awesome location. Very close to Byron and Tweed Heads plenty of things to do. Definitely would come back and stay at Ferry reserve.

Reflections went out of their way to make our anniversary such a wonderful stay. Flowers, champagne and chocolates on arrival. What a  lovely surprise. Wow thanks team you really excelled yourself. Always lovely to stay but this time , just perfect.
Clean, quiet and oh so nice.

The ferry reserve is a great little park right on the river and it's a perfect stop to go and relax,fish,crab and just read a book. The staff are more than accommodating with anything you need. Would totally recommend staying here for a week or 3
Nice spot on the river to chill out in

Clean, well-equipped cabin right opposite the river. Great ensuite shower and comfortable bed with good quality linen. Kitchenette had everything we needed. Dogs are welcome here and the common areas are spotless.

What a great place to stay! A choice of a tiny house or a 2 bedroom house facing the river. So relaxing and peaceful. We have been there several times and would recommend it to anyone. A short drive to the shopping centre where there are several places to buy food or eat out. The accommodation is very clean with everthing inside you might need. The beach is close by at the junction of the river. An easy booking system and friendly staff as well.

What an amazing spot to stay staff are very friendly an helpful all amenities are so clean a nice quiet spot close to shops pubs shopping centre all up the road close an handy. an the river not far away second time staying here we absolutely love it an will be back
Beautifully maintained. Amenities were clean and tidy. Dog friendly and.walks were a breeze. At least three different environments for my dog to walk and play. Definitely going back.
Beautiful place to stay with your family and the furry ones. Definitely a place to come back again

We stayed here for 3 nights recently.
On arrival we was pretty suprised at how the place looked.  Closed road alongside the park with a derelict building.
Lots of permanent residents. Not an overly attractive park to stay.
We asked for a water front site but was advised that these were not suitable for caravans as they didn't want an obstructions in front of the cabins.
So we had to settle for a corner powered site.
On walking around the park later we noticed a large shabby bus looking vehicle parked at the waterfront camping. Which was not far off the size of a caravan????

Apart from the above, the location of this park is ideal for a stroll into Brunswick.
The amenities and camp kitchens were kept very clean.
Make sure you take your mozzie and bug repellent, though as you will need it.

I do feel this park is mega overpriced $75 per night for a powered site.
3 hours after arriving we were advised the power would be off for 4 hours due to a faulty power pole close by. We could have been advised of this at reception beforehand.
But if you have a dog this is the only park that accepts them so had no choice for the area.

I dont think we will be visiting again.

Myself, my mother and her dog had the best holiday here in October. We stayed in the tiny house option. Check in was at 2:00pm but we arrived at 11:00am after a 6.5 hour drive they let us check in early which we were so grateful for. It’s right on the river which is amazing in itself! Beautiful views of mountains over the river too. Has river access we went swimming everyday, the water is so clear. The grounds are clean, the people are friendly, it has useful amenities like a laundry room to do your washing. The tiny house had tvs, air con, double bed which was nice, fully equipped kitchen, shower, and toilet. The deck had river views and outdoor seating. Also we couldn’t hear any noise from the highway. Already planning our next holiday here, can’t wait to go back!

Camped by the river. Beautiful!
Cabins appeared modern, bright and well situated close to river.
Toilets/showers clean and secure
Camp kitchen clean.
Great park and very nice staff.
Brilliant place dog friendly great staff close to walk to shops
Fabulous one night stay in a tiny home. Had everything you could need and more. Heating and aircon also. Definitely coming back and staff lovely.
Such a stunning park. We were visiting friends. So good to be able to take their beautiful little dog 🐕
Perfect
Great spot on the river l plan to return next trip up north

We had a one night stay at the premium unpowered camp site. The site was beautiful with stunning river+mountain view. The staff was very friendly and amenities were spotless clean! There was indeed some noises from the highway but not a huge issue if you have ear plugs.
Very clean, great staff but next to noisy highway.

Always stay here when passing .
Love this park.
Great 3 day stay in a tiny house. Dog friendly and having a gate on the deck was brilliant.

Very welcoming and helpful staff, park is in excellent condition, camp kitchen clean and tidy. Amenities block modern, with everything you need. Toilets clean and showers hot. Swimming or sitting by the river was lovely. 30 mins walk to town

Easy to get to, i.e. close to motorway northbound. But the traffic noise didn't bother us. Many types of accommodation. We got a powered riverfront campsite. There's a walking route along the river to shops & eateries. And a bush walking track the other way along the river. Dog friendly. Tidy grounds. Well maintained amenities. Ticked all our boxes.

We stayed with our little dog two nights in a tiny house and loved it. The tiny houses appear new and are thoughtfully designed. They are very well equipped. The huge deck is dog proof and is a great spot to sit and watch the river glide by. Yes, even water views! Very relaxing and peaceful spot to stay.

My wife Deb and I and our 2 dogs stayed at reflection caravan park for 2 weeks in our van .What a great spot with clean facilities and a very neat and tidy  park .Book in was very fast and easy .All staff in the office and the yardmen and cleaners were very friendly and helpful .The few permanent resident  homes and vans were very tidy and well maintained .Great park we will be back .⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Tiny house is just so cosy and comfortable and a beautiful outlook of the river. Highly recommend Ferry Reserve for a relaxing getaway.....Dog friendly is a plus also and a lovely walk into Brunswick Heads town along the river. All the facilities are very good and looked after.
Stayed many times. Still OK.

Great place to camp with dogs
Very clean facilities and all well maintained, little bit of noise from the highway but all in all a great experience

Dog friendly which is fantastic! Beautiful , comfy, clean cabin and surroundings. So pretty with lake views and lots of bird life, nice walks and short car trips to beautiful Nth NSW beaches with dog beaches close by as well. Peaceful lovely park we love staying at Reflections Ferry Reserve.

This park is beautiful! It has everything! You can paddle board, you can kayak, you can ride your bike into town, the view of the river is amazing! The sunsets are spectacular!! The amenities are clean & well maintained as are the gardens etc. But after being booked in for 2 nights, we left after only one. It’s the first time I’ve ever left a camp early. The M1 noise was relentless & loud when camping in a tent. And then in the morning the jet skis & tinnies start. I’m a heavy sleeper from the middle of the city & I think I got a max of 2 hours sleep.

We discovered the Reflection Group on our last motorhome trip and we can say that they are very organised. In particular, this campground overlooks the Brunswick River and offers an oasis of tranquillity especially for those who enjoy fishing. There is space for double slab motorhomes, but there are also other options. Dog friendly. Very clean, fragrant toilets and common areas available with all amenities. The bad thing is that it is only a few kilometres from the beaches, but if you prefer to walk in 30 minutes you are on the coast.

Beautiful location next to the river. Lovely sunsets over the distant hills. Most of the caravan sites are on a double slab, great idea. Amenities are clean and modern. Motorway is close but does not detract from this great park.

The park is lovely right by the river |Amenities very clean and fresh|Staff were very friendly and helpful very relaxing holiday People in the park were nice to talk to and short trip into town for nice lunches and dinners at the Brunswick hotel
Big family gathering,  plenty of room, large undercover BBQ area was great and clean,  amenities always clean

An awesome place to stay, we had the self-contained bungalow. It was super clean and the staff were very friendly and helpful at all times. A little out of town but only a short drive to anywhere you want to go. Thanks very much and will definitely be back again.

Wondered why the park was so empty = war zone !!! the truck noise is relentless. I lived next to the eastern freeway in Melbourne but this was far worse , air brakes then exellerate then repeat every 20 seconds. Sad considering its riverside location and being dog friendly. Had 2 dogs so we were desperate and other parks in the area don't allow dogs. Ho Hum 😒
P.S earplugs Should be issued with key code .
Basic & not cheap. So many people but we'll behaved & quiet . Nice n close to Bruns, beautiful on the river.

Perfect for our family (2 adults & 2 year old)!!
Plus grandparents in a camper van site down a bit.

We were at powered camp site #1 & it was the best location for us, right next to the camp kitchen, which had a fridge, microwave, kettle, toaster & BBQs.
Big bins everywhere for convenience & they didn’t smell at all.
Also near the toilets/showers, bathrooms etc were so fresh & cleaned regularly & the shower water was hot!
Also was a big enough site that next time we will have the grandparents in the camper van on it, plus our 3 dome tent setup, as we will all easily fit.
Was great having everything close by when you have a toddler, but not too close, sound wise.

Speaking of sound, I saw a lot of negative reviews on here, before going, about the highway noise overnight, after going, I can confirm there is some noise from big trucks overnight, but it’s really not that bad.
I would suggest bringing your own ear plugs if you’re a light sleeper/need complete silence overnight.
But location wise it’s near the highway so what do you expect.
To us who use a white noise machine at home, it was similar so was completely fine with us.

Check in/out was super fast & simple, I can’t remember the ladies name at the reception desk, but she was so helpful & nice.
We had spontaneously booked this trip to surprise the grandparents, so we got a site close to them & she texted us to let us know when they were checking in, so we could go surprise them, as we got there a few hours before them.

Beach area down the left of the camp grounds & river, was nice & secluded for toddler to play in safely with us.

Can’t wait to come back more & especially summer time!

Value for money it is really bad, one of the most expensive caravan park I have stayed in, out of town, not close to the beach, with no pool, very poor boat ramp only good at high tide but it is very close to highway. Before you pay a deposit make sure you don't get sick or any reason you cannot go, as they have a no refund policy.
Beautiful place, great price and friendly staff. Will go again.

Stayed her for4 night powered,slab site spacious good price. Close to walk into town and river very close by. Highly recommend Ferry Reserve plus we have re booked for on our way home from Lake Broadwater. 👍

Booked a campsite for 2 nights. First time staying here. Whilst the park is well appointed and the river is pristine, the highway being in such close proximity was much too noisy. All night it is impossible sleep through the noise of the trucks airbrakes and the highway traffic. Very disappointed. Packed up and left after one night. Perhaps if you are staying in a cabin or caravan, you may be able to be insulated from the noise a little.
Will not be returning. Expensive for a campsite.

Potential to be a beautiful little camping spot, but it is ruined by the proximity to the Pacific Highway. The sound of trucks using engine brakes, then accelerating ALL NIGHT is unrelenting and makes for an unpleasant experience.

The tent sites also seem to have drainage issues if there is any rain about with many sites having to deal with ponding water in or around their tent / campervans.

It's a shame, as the amenities were clean and tidy, the camp kitchen is well appointed and the river is so beautiful and would be so serene if it weren't for the highway noise.

I would not camp here again.
Nice park, but is cashless.... Tried to pay cash just for some bait and they wanted card.
Stayed solo at a Little Cabin - perfect for my 3 night stay. Clean.  Modern and comfortable.  Supplied with all you need for a top visit  to Bruns and beyond. Will return.
Gorgeous and calming peacful and perfect location.

A very tidy park!
We thought at $81 per night it was a bit rich considering we just spent 4 weeks on the road and it was the most we paid.

It's always fantastic to come home to Bruns & stay at Reflections Massey Greene.
Staff are great, especially Webby,  top bloke, cannot do enough for you, always has time to chat & share a ginger beer.
Jarrod is a great bloke, Lorna is just crazy, and they both always have a smile 😃.
We are exceptionally grateful to be holidaying in the park after losing our site to the development of the cabins after 27yrs.
Lorna made the transition simply painless for us & again, we are very grateful.
Highly recommend spending time at Massey, short term or long term, there's always a greeting smile, fun times for all ages.
Simply kick back & enjoy what both the park / township & surrounding area have to offer.
Beautiful spot although very wet everywhere at the moment the staff are friendly and helpful and it’s clean quiet and pet friendly

Great location right on Bruns river and offering great quality clean cabins, tiny houses and public facilities.
The staff are friendly and helpful and so whatever they can to help with bookings and changes I needed to make with my bookings.

We stay here often when our own house is rented out and always feel welcome. It’s one of the very few parks that welcomes dogs and it’s great to see that they do t plan to change that.

There are some great access points at to the river and a boat ramp for small boats, tinnies at the park. Easy walking g distance to Bruns village.
Was disappointed as no walking tracks, very close to highway and could hear the traffic night and day.
Stayed in one of the excellent cabins.  Clean and comfortable
Beautiful dog friendly caravan parks
Great van park.

Very tidy caravan park located on the river
Our site had easy access and most sites had concrete slabs
The amenities were modern and well serviced
Lovely spot by the water. Clean, friendly, and pet friendly! plus a nice walk to the town. Thanks for the stay!
Beautiful place.
On the river. Very relaxed. Facilities great and little house was perfect. 😊

Park very clean and tidy, the young lady at reception was very friendly and helpful, bit close to the highway but didn't stop me from sleeping.
Would stay there again.

Highly Recommend - I stayed in one of the Tiny Homes.

- Really great cabin with everything you need, including stove, fridge, microwave, nice linens, shower and toilet, cosy heating and air-con
- easy to enjoy the outdoors with a great little deck and views across River.
- staff were all helpful and lovely
- Dog friendly
- 15 min nature walk to township with cafes etc
- dog friendly beach at Brunswick is maybe 25 min walk or so.

Others have complained about the noise on their reviews. You are close to the highway so there was a white noise constantly. I’ve lived in the city so found it similar and forgot about it quickly. Cabin sheltered much of that noise at night.
